name: Cook's Treat Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the stuffing by toasting the oatmeal bread until medium brown. Allow it to cool slightly. Chop the scallions into pieces about 1/4-to-1/2 inch long. Slice or quarter the button mushrooms if they are small. Roughly chop the parsley and cut the toast into pieces about 1/2 inch square.
2. Combine all stuffing ingredients in a large mixing bowl, add the beaten eggs and mix well. Season with salt to taste and pepper if desired. Add Bell's Poultry Seasoning for extra flavor (optional).
3. Reserve some of the stuffing (about a quarter or third) and add chopped giblets to it, as many people prefer them not in the stuffing.
4. Stuff the cleaned and dried chicken with the non-gibletted stuffing.
5. Preheat the oven to between 350°F and 400°F. Apply butter evenly on the chicken's surface and season with salt.
6. Place the stuffed chicken breast side up on a roasting rack set in a pan with sides about an inch high (a large Pyrex cake pan works well). Use a suitable rack, such as one with adjustable sides.
7. With a fork or chopsticks, spread the gibletted stuffing over the chicken surface, pressing it into place. Place the neck within reach for basting. Dot the stuffing generously with small butter pats (optional).
8. Put the bird in the oven and close the door. While waiting about 15 minutes, chat with guests and serve wine.
9. After 15 minutes, begin basting the chicken regularly every five to ten minutes using a bulb baster. Season occasionally with salt. The stuffing will brown as it absorbs buttery flavors from the chicken juices and steam releases from the mushrooms.
10. Keep basting until the surface of the chicken is fully coated in golden-brown crispy toast bits, indicating readiness for serving. Carefully remove the chicken, transfer to a platter, and de-stuff.
11. Serve with rolls, salads, vegetables, mashed potatoes, and gravy made from instant potatoes. Typically, I finish off a leg, wing, or neck for visual appeal, but you can eat at your leisure.
Warnings:
- Avoid using garlic as it disrupts the dish's flavor balance (heresy to some).
- Do not substitute oatmeal bread with other types, which may require additional seasoning adjustments.
